name: Tallinn
author: ahkimn
description:
# Tallinn ### TLL · v0.2.1 Part of the [EU Map Pack](https://ahkimn.github.io/subwaybuilder-eu-maps). Highly-detailed map of the Tallinn metropolitan area using 2021 Estonian government statistics. ![Map Coverage](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Subway-Builder-Modded/registry/refs/heads/main/maps/yukina-ee-tallinn/gallery/preview.webp) ## Coverage

## Additional Features - **Building Collision** — A buildings index is included for all non-filtered buildings, each carrying a per-building foundation depth in in-game meters. Depths scale with building height and footprint width and are bounded to the **−10 m** to **−80 m** range: single-family homes and low-rise structures anchor at the floor, while supertall towers with deep bedrock piles (Taipei-101 class) reach the cap. Freestanding towers, masts, chimneys, and other slender non-occupiable structures are detected by shape and floored to the **−10 m** minimum. Foundation depths drive underground tunnel-clearance collision and are also emitted as a visual `foundations` pmtiles layer visible on the in-game map. Building geometry and floor counts come from the EHR national building cache with the Overture Maps Foundation footprint side-channel as the height fallback. - **Water Depth Index** — A water depth index restricts track placement in water bodies. Coastal bundles include a real bathymetric layer derived from EMODnet Bathymetry (1/16 arc-min DTM, ~115 m resolution); inland water (rivers, lakes, canals) uses a flat **−5 m** index that prevents surface-level track placement. - **Ocean Foundations** — A depth-colored ocean floor layer is included for coastal bundles, providing a visual representation of Baltic Sea bathymetry visible on the in-game map. - **Place Name Labels** — Three layers of place name labels are embedded in the map: - _City labels_ — Estonian linn (city-class KOVs) at the metropolitan scale. - _Municipality labels_ — Names of all omavalitsus (KOVs — linn and vald) within the map boundary. - _Neighborhood labels_ — Sub-municipal settlement names (asustusüksus for rural areas, plus asum / kvartal / linnaosa for the AKS-subdivided cities of Tallinn, Tartu, Pärnu, Narva, and Kohtla-Järve). ## Methodology This map is generated through a six-phase pipeline that transforms Estonian government census, building, and employment statistics into georeferenced origin/destination demand points. **Point Seeding:** Candidate points are placed within each _asustusüksus_ (settlement unit) using population-weighted centroids derived from a hybrid 100 m / 250 m mesh — Statistikaamet's INSPIRE-aligned gridded population at native resolution where available, dasymetrically refined against the EHR (Ehitisregister) national building cache + per-building KAOS use codes (residential filter + apartment unit-count cascade). **Mesh Reconciliation:** Initial point populations are refined by solving a constrained convex optimization that approximates each point's mass to residential and economic mesh ideals while preserving REL 2021 (Eesti rahvaloendus 2021) totals for each KOV. The five largest cities (Tallinn, Tartu, Pärnu, Narva, Kohtla-Järve) are further subdivided into asum / kvartal / linnaosa neighborhoods via Maa-amet's official AKS Mitteametlikud piirkonnad boundaries. **Aggregation:** A global scaling factor reconciles the mismatch between boundary-captured employed residents (from REL 2021) and workplace employees. Workplace mass is derived from the EHR cache filtered through a three-tier KAOS dispatch (residential / workplace / mixed-use), with per-firm FTE rollups from the Eesti Ametlik Ariregister (Estonian Commercial Register) annual reports providing the ground-truth workplace anchor. **Commute Assignment:** Commute flows are assigned using a gravity model with distance-decay, calibrated against the REL 2021 RL21163 commute matrix (residence × workplace bin-marginal shares). The five-bin projection (own-KOV / same-maakond / cross-maakond / Tallinn-pin / Tartu-pin) is reconciled via Generalized IPF with destination-pinning on the two anchor cities. A symmetric containment-share guard prevents over-routing to the urban core. **Special Demand Injection:** Non-commuter demand generators — airports, passenger ports, universities, hospitals, military installations, and tourism attractions — are injected as an additive layer. **Layout Reconciliation:** A final spatial pass resolves point overlaps and ensures map clickability while keeping special-demand locations fixed. ## Data Sources All demand modeling is derived from freely available Estonian government statistical datasets and public geospatial resources. ### Census & Demographics - **2021 Census** (_Eesti rahvaloendus 2021_ / REL 2021 — per-municipality and per-asustusüksus population, employment, and demographics) — [Statistikaamet / Statistics Estonia](https://www.stat.ee/) - **INSPIRE Population Grid** (RR21 — per-100 m and per-250 m gridded population, INSPIRE-aligned in EPSG:3035) — [Statistikaamet](https://www.stat.ee/) - **Commute O/D at KOV level** (RL21163 — residence × workplace bin-marginal shares: own-KOV / same-maakond / cross-maakond / Tallinn / Tartu) — [Statistikaamet](https://www.stat.ee/) - **Workplace Employment Statistics** (RL21166 — employed residents by EMTAK economic activity × residence county; per-firm employee counts from Eesti Ametlik Ariregister annual reports) — [Statistikaamet](https://www.stat.ee/) · [Äriregister](https://ariregister.rik.ee/) ### Geospatial Reference - **Administrative Boundaries** (EHAK — _Eesti haldus- ja asustusjaotuse klassifikaator_ — omavalitsus + maakond + asustusüksus polygons) — [Statistikaamet EHAK](https://www.stat.ee/en/find-statistics/methodology/classifications/ehak) - **Building Registry** (EHR — _Ehitisregister_ — national building cache with per-building KAOS use codes, floor counts, footprints, and unit counts) — [Eesti Ehitisregister](https://www.ehr.ee/) - **Building Use Taxonomy** (KAOS — _Ehitise kasutamise otstarvete loetelu_ — official taxonomy of building-use codes by Ehitisregister) — [Maa-amet](https://geoportaal.maaamet.ee/) - **Topographic Polygons** (ETAK — _Eesti Topograafiline Andmekogu_ — building footprints + land-use + water + transport ROW polygons) — [Maa-amet](https://geoportaal.maaamet.ee/) - **Address Data System** (ADS — _Aadressiandmete süsteem_ — Maa-amet's authoritative geocoding service for institutional addresses) — [Maa-amet ADS](https://aadressid.maaamet.ee/) - **Cadastre Snapshot** (KATASTER — annual Maa-amet cadastral parcel snapshot with siht1 zoning classifications) — [Maa-amet](https://geoportaal.maaamet.ee/) - **AKS Mitteametlikud piirkonnad** (Maa-amet's authoritative sub-KOV subdivision for asum / kvartal / linnaosa / island / osavald — Tallinn 92 polygons, Tartu 51, Pärnu 22, Narva 19, Kohtla-Järve 5) — [Maa-amet](https://geoportaal.maaamet.ee/) - **Bathymetry** (EMODnet Bathymetry DTM, ~115 m resolution — coastal bundles only) — [EMODnet Bathymetry Consortium](https://emodnet.ec.europa.eu/en/bathymetry) - **Overture Maps Foundation** (auxiliary building footprint side-channel for the EHR height cascade + Phase E POI) — [Overture](https://overturemaps.org/) ### Special Demand Statistics - **Airport Passenger Statistics** (terminal-level annual passengers) — [Tallinna Lennujaam / Tallinn Airport](https://www.tallinn-airport.ee/) - **Passenger-Ferry Terminal Statistics** (per-port annual passenger throughput — coastal bundles only: Tallinn / Helsinki-Tallinn / Paldiski / Sillamäe / Saaremaa) — [Tallinna Sadam / Port of Tallinn](https://www.ts.ee/) · [Statistikaamet](https://www.stat.ee/) - **University Registry** (EHIS / _Eesti Hariduse Infosüsteem_ — institutional metadata + per-institution enrollment) — [HTM / EHIS](https://www.haridussilm.ee/) - **Hospital Registry & Bed Statistics** (Tervise Arengu Instituut / National Institute for Health Development — 20 Hospital Network Development Plan hospitals with per-facility annual bed counts, occupancy rates, and outpatient walk-in visit counts from tables HH01 / HH04 / HH08, vintage 2023) — [TAI Health Statistics Database](https://statistika.tai.ee/pxweb/en/Andmebaas/) · [Sotsiaalministeerium — Haiglavõrgu arengukava](https://www.sm.ee/haiglavork-ja-haiglavorgu-arengukavad) - **Military Installations** (curated 18-row garrison roster: Kaitsevägi + NATO Enhanced Forward Presence + NATO CCDCOE + Kaitseliit — per-unit personnel estimates cross-validated against mil.ee unit pages; Kaitseministeerium HQ and Kaitseväe Akadeemia staff are attributed through the workplace pipeline instead). Base perimeters extracted from OpenStreetMap `landuse=military` and `military=training_area/base/airfield/naval_base/range` polygons to prevent double-counting between workplace mass and Phase E special demand — [Kaitsevägi / Estonian Defence Forces](https://mil.ee/en/) · [Kaitseliit / Estonian Defence League](https://kaitseliit.ee/en/) · [NATO CCDCOE](https://ccdcoe.org/) · [NATO eFP Battlegroup Estonia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NATO_Enhanced_Forward_Presence) · [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/) - **Tourism Visitor Statistics** (per-attraction visitor counts — Statistikaamet TUR042 + per-site annual reports) — [Statistikaamet TUR042](https://www.stat.ee/) · [Visit Estonia](https://www.visitestonia.com/) ## License This map data is released under the [GNU General Public License v3.0](https://github.com/ahkimn/subwaybuilder-eu-maps/blob/main/LICENSE). ## Credits Map authored by [Yukina-](https://subwaybuildermodded.com/credits/)
